permitted_uses.use_name: The only permitted uses in this zone include single detached dwellings, additions, or accessory buildings are permitted according to Section 3.1.

provisions.text: There are ample undeveloped lands to the north of the TransCanada Highway near Winsloe and East Royalty in Charlottetown. Future expansion of the City's urban fabric should feature a mix of residential densities and Complete Communities which should be proactively planned instead of piecemeal developed. Many of these properties are large and would benefit from Secondary Planning to coordinate a variety of land owners.
Rezoning from UE to any other zone is only permitted through a secondary planning process.
The only permitted uses in this zone include single detached dwellings, additions, or accessory buildings are permitted according to Section 3.1.

Subdivisions are only permitted through a development agreement or secondary planning process consistent with the secondary plan recommendations. Secondary planning will take into account more than one land owner to establish main transportation connections, park needs, mix of uses that meet the local demand, conservation measures, and the future routing of services across multiple properties.
Up to one single detached dwelling on an existing lot may be considered following the RN Zone requirements.
